entry.title: Seeking Guidance on SAP Commercial/Finance Reporting, WBS Creations, and Coresystem
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Joshua Stephen and I'm new to this community. I am writing to request your assistance as I embark on my journey to learn SAP, with a focus on Commercial/Finance Reporting, WBS Creations, and Field Service Management in the Coresystem.

As a beginner, I am finding it challenging due to the lack of socialization and awareness in my current environment and community. I am eager to improve my skills and knowledge in these areas and would greatly appreciate any help, guidance, or resources you might be able to provide.

Looking forward to learning from all of you.

entry.title: Missing Movement Type 169
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a return STO to process in MIGO using movement type 169.

The problem is that this movement is not showing in the dropdown list. In OMJJ I have matched the settings with other visible movements, and still I cannot see or select this mvt after saving the settings. What am I missing? What else do I need to change so that I can see this mvt in the dropdown list?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Disable HANA System Replication to have a second node to another Standard Hana install
entry.content:
Hi,

Nowadays we have a PERSON_NAME System Repplication from Host A to Host B.

We have plans to STOP the System Repplication and we would like to have 2 PERSON_NAME DB install, on Host A and also on Host B.

We would like to have to PERSON_NAME DB.

How can we do it ? We do not like to have Backup/Restore because ther servers are not in the same LAN.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Disable HANA System Replication to have a second node...
entry.content:
"
Nowadays we have a HANA System Repplication from Host A to Host B.

We have plans to STOP the System Repplication and we would like to have 2 HANA DB install, on Host A and also on Host B.

We would like to have to HANA DB.

How can we do it ? We do not like to have Backup/Restore because ther servers are not in the same LAN.
"

You mean that host B which is now secondary will become a standalone but you want it to have the data the same as primary (up to the point when replication was disabled)
This is certainly fine and a support way to make a copy of the primary system.

SAP HANA System Replication Guide
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4e9b18c116aa42fc84c7dbfd02111aba/38ad53e538ad41db9d12d22a6c8f2503.html

See Scenario 1 of
1945676 - Correct usage of hdbnsutil -sr_unregister
https://me.sap.com/notes/1945676/E

Do note that once the host B becomes unregistered, it will not be covered by the license from primary, so you will need to request and install a new license for it.
2213293 - How to request SAP HANA Platform or Enterprise Edition license keys - SAP for Me
https://me.sap.com/notes/2213293

entry.title: Re: SORTING IN WOCR
entry.content:
Hello @former_member844305 , 

Sort Rule in Packing Profile arrange the Warehouse Tasks within a particular Warehouse Order based on Sort fields which you have defined in customizing lets say based on Loading weight, so based on this , WT will be sorted within WO and will be presented to RF operator 

For example if you have 3 products say EWM-01, EWM-02, EWM-03 loading weight corresponding 1 , 2 , 3 KG

Lets say you created a SORT Rule which has field Loading Weight and you arrange the field in decending order 

So in this case when WT will be created to to pick each qty 1 then system will check Sort Rule assigned to Packing Profile and since here you have in decending so in RF , warehouse operator will be asked to pick EWM-03 first as loading weight is 3KG , then EWM-02 as loading weight is 2 KG and then EWM-01 as loading weight is 1KG

Br 

Shazeb

In the tutorial it gives some troubleshooting, and for 500 it says:

entry.title: Simplifying Custom SAP UI5 Library Consumption in SAP BAS
entry.content:
In the realm of SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), the journey of integrating custom UI5 libraries into your projects can sometimes feel like navigating through a labyrinth. As a developer, I recently encountered this challenge firsthand while working on a project that required frequent deployment of a custom UI5 library. Each deployment was not only time-consuming but also seemed unnecessary given the local resources at hand.

Upon delving into the depths of the SAP BAS ecosystem and scouring through community insights, I stumbled upon a revelation: the solution might be closer than anticipated. Initially inspired by @MioYasuatke's blog post on How to consume UI5 custom libraries in BAS or VS Code?, I embarked on a quest to streamline this process further. Little did I know that SAP BAS had already laid the groundwork for simplifying this intricate task just like we had in SAP WebIDE.

 

Unveiling the Magic of SAP BAS

In SAP Business Application Studio, the process of linking or consuming custom SAPUI5 libraries is a breeze, provided you know where to look and how to wield the tools at your disposal. Let's break down the steps:

1. Fiori Add Reference to SAP Fiori Reusable Libraries: Navigate to the Command Palette and execute this command. A prompt will emerge, prompting you to select the desired local libraries from your workspace. Once selected, a simple click on "Finish" sets the wheels in motion.

Command Palette

 
SAP Fiori Reuseable Library wizard
 
 
2. Manifest.json and ui5.yaml: With a flick of the wand, SAP BAS updates the manifest.json and ui5.yaml files seamlessly, paving the way for a harmonious integration.
 
myApplication's manifest file

myApplication's ui5-yaml file

3. A Minor Hiccup: However, as with any magical endeavor, a small hiccup ensued. Attempting to run the application via npm run start led to an error. The culprit? The application struggled to locate the requisite files within the designated path.

 

first npm run start error
 
 

Mastering the ui5.yaml and ui5/cli Commands

Fear not, for every setback is merely an opportunity to unravel the mysteries of the ui5.yaml file and the power of ui5/cli commands.

1. Understanding Source and Build Code: In the realm of application development, code is bifurcated into source and build code. While source code houses our logic, build code represents its minified counterpart. The error encountered during npm run start stemmed from the search for minified files in the "/src" directory, leading us to our next step.

2. Executing ui5 build: By invoking the ui5 build command in the terminal, we initiate the transformation of source code into its minified form. Voila! The "/dist" folder now houses the coveted minified files.

ui5 build library

dist folder

NOTE: the build path can be changed in package.json file. But its better to build the files at /dist directory.

3. Mapping Paths: With the minified code at our disposal, it's imperative to update the manifest.json file within the SAP UI5 application. By providing the relative path to the library's "/dist" folder, we ensure seamless integration.

updated myApplication's ui5-yaml file

 

Now if you execute npm run start for UI5 application everything works fine!

Congratulations!! 🎉

You now can load custom SAP UI5 library from your local workspace without worrying to remove any temp code everytime during deployment!

 

Conclusion: Empowered by Knowledge

With SAP Business Application Studio's intuitive features and the understanding of key files like manifest.json and ui5.yaml, consuming #custom UI5 libraries becomes a breeze. By following these steps, you can streamline your development workflow and focus on building innovative UI5 applications without the headache of repetitive deployment tasks. Congratulations on simplifying your library deployment process

entry.title: Getting exception while calling the MessageProcessingLogs from CPI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I was trying to call the Message processing logs from CPI in cloud foundry. But i am getting the below exception 

com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: An exception of type 'WstxUnexpectedCharException' occurred., cause: com.ctc.wstx.exc.WstxUnexpectedCharException: Unexpected character ';' (code 59) excepted space, or '>' or "/>"

I am using the client id and client secret as basic Authentication. Please suggest me on this.

entry.title: Sap analytic cloud aggregation
entry.content:
Hi

I have this complex data that present WBS , PO and GR and SD at the same data

Now i want to show the WBS budget that calculate from the PO amount

i have many rows with the same amount because there are more variables in the data
The Unique combination for the WBS budget is to sum the PO number with PO product and PO serial

when i make another measure agg  on those three its show the same number as the PO amount measure

this is a sample of the data

hope you can help me with that i am really stuck 

Thank you

entry.title: Build service failed community edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

Are there issues with the build service today? I updated my web app last week no issues. After a small update today, build service failed. Any insight would be greatly appreciated.

Runtime version: 4.9.148;
AB version: 2.0.0-alpha.1
COMMAND:

/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@appgyver/orchestra-builder/node_modules/rnv/bin/index.jsconfigure -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @appgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.148 --hooks --npxMode

FAILED with ERROR:

Command failed with exit code 1: /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@appgyver/orchestra-builder/node_modules/rnv/bin/index.js configure -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @appgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.148 --hooks --npxMode

entry.title: Re: IBP - Editable key figure only editable on lowest (ba...
entry.content:
Hi @vincentverheyen, another idea would be to create:

- Dummy simple master data entry for the base planning level (Location in your example)
- Planning objects for the dummy MD entry on the base PL
- Permission filter with option Location <> DUMMY and assign to users

When changing values on Month + Product, IBP will try to disaggregate them to Month + Product + Location and give authorization issue. This proposal depends on KF disaggregation settings (can confirm for Equal Distribution / Same Key Figure - Stored Values).

entry.title: sap system issue analysis by kernel snapshot analyzer tool
entry.content:
Overview

In this blog post, I've detailed the process of utilizing the SAP Kernel Snapshot Analyzer tool through the examination of server snapshots.

DISCLAIMER

The content of this blog post is provided “AS IS”. This information could contain technical inaccuracies, typographical errors, and out-of-date information. This document may be updated or changed without notice at any time. Use of the information is therefore at your own risk. In no event shall SAP be liable for special, indirect, incidental, or consequential damages resulting from or related to the use of this document.

Purpose           

This tool can be used for troubleshooting server issue kernel kernel-related issues.

Mainly tool used for dispatcher scheduler information, queue statistics, work process table, session table, work process call stacks, PROC memory consumption, and the root cause analysis for SAP Kernel-related issues.

Prerequisites

SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP is a feature available from SAP Kernel 740.

The application server can be configured to automatically or manually generate a dump of critical information when the system encounters abnormal situations, such as handling requests without progress. This dump includes vital details about the server's state and processes at the time of the incident.

SAP Kernel snapshot analysis steps: –

  • Generate snapshot
  • Download snapshot
  • Download kernel analyzer
  • Upload snapshot and generate kernel snapshot analysis report

Generate snapshot:-

SAP system snapshot can automatically be generated when WP unexpectedly finished, hard shutdown, exhausted resources, lack of WP, etc.

Manually generate snapshots via SAP GUI and via OS.

Snapshot generated via SAP GUI

SAP transaction: SM50 -> Administration->Snapshot -> Create

OS level generates snapshots

Run demon from OS and follow the below command to create the snapshot

m - menu -> p - work-process-adm-table -> e - create snapshot

 

Download snapshot

You can obtain the snapshot file from the SAP system, which is a compressed archive containing ample application server information for analysis. You can utilize the following method to download snapshot files:

SM50 -> Administration->Snapshot -> Administration

Select the snapshot and download it into Dashtop

You can download the snapshot files directly from the operating system

OS snapshot path:-  /usr/sap/<System ID>/SYS/global/sapcontrol/snapshots/

Download kernel analyzer

The Kernel Snapshot Analyzer is a specialized tool crafted for diagnosing abnormal behaviors within system snapshots. These snapshots can be sourced from the snapshot file or the work directory (developer traces) of the SAP system.

Before proceeding with the analysis, ensure that your system has the Java Runtime Environment (JRE) installed correctly. It's recommended to have at least JRE7 available.

To utilize the Kernel Snapshot Analyzer, follow these steps:

  1. Download the executable file KernelSnapshotAnalyzer.jar.
  2. Also, acquire the introduction slides for guidance.
  3. Once downloaded, run the KernelSnapshotAnalyzer.jar file on your local machine.

By running this tool, you can efficiently diagnose and address any anomalies present within the server snapshots, thereby ensuring optimal performance and stability of your SAP system.

Download the tool from the below link:-

Download kernel snapshot analyzer

 

Upload snapshot and generate kernel snapshot analysis report:-

Run KernelSnapshotAnalyzer.jar and upload the downloaded snapshot into the tool  

Like below:-

Run analysis report:- it will generate an XML report like below

C-call stacks for all types of work processes:

 

 

 

Detailed analysis available for all below points

 

Reference SAP Notes:-

 2640476 - How to analyze Server Snapshot with kernel snapshot analyzer.

 3110588 - Kernel Snapshot Analyzer: Central KBA

Advantages:-

  •     Helpful for troubleshooting unexpected shutdown
  •     Help for Call stacks related to all snapshot DIA processes
  •     Kernel parameters details and corrections if needed

 

I hope this document will help you with the sap kernel snapshot analyzer tool.

 

Cheers!

Anikesh Jyotishi

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am Zora, a teacher by profession, a 47 years old woman who wants to switch her career. I am passionate about IT and sales. In today's fast-paced digital world, upgrading skills is crucial. This initiative caught my attention as it offers a structured way to enhance digital skills. I believe this will boost my career. I am confident that this will help me apply the acquired knowledge effectively. My interest in this initiative stems from a desire to stay updated in the digital field and contribute meaningfully to the industry. I am excited to be part of this transformative journey. Thank you for the opportunity.

entry.title: Re: How track Fiori App usage in S/4 Hana On-Premise
entry.content:
Hi, There is a custom tool I've had a hand in developing. It is called the Fiori App Usage Report and addresses most of the requests from the question. The details are on the product page: https://help.fioriappsusage.org/.

I hope it helps
Greg Malewski

entry.title: Join the Livestream of Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum
entry.content:
Our Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum Live Kickoff starts at 8:30 am ET and streams all day until 5:30 pm ET. Join us on the Livestream: https://broadcast.sap.com/go/nextgensapea

See below for our session schedule and more event details.

On February 27th we will be live streaming Day 1 of the plenary track and track 1 sessions for the SAP Community Enterprise Architect Group 

 

This is being done to help Enterprise Architects in the SAP Community since the event is now sold out and especially to help organizations that maybe under travel restrictions and cannot attend. 

Our special Live Stream “Pregame Show” starts at 8:30 am Eastern Time. 

 

Our Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum Live Kickoff starts at 8:30 am ET and we will broadcast live until 5:30 pm ET. Access the live stream anytime during the day at this link: https://broadcast.sap.com/go/nextgensapea

Here are the sessions that will be livestreamed on February 27th: 

  • The Future Does Not Fit in the Containers of the Past 
  • Better. Transform. Together. Accelerating Business Transformation with EA 
  • Turning Strategy Into Reality With Business Architecture
  • The Cloud & AI Frontier: Navigating Strategy, Architecture, Migration and Innovation for a Cloud World 
  • The Enterprise Architect’s Blueprint for SAP S/4HANA Success 
  • Discover SAP BTP’s Strategy and Roadmap Highlights
  • Unpacking the Transformation Suite Approach and Toolchain 
  • Simplifying your Data Readiness and Migration for SAP S/4HANA, Cloud & AI
  • SAP Signavio: Next Generation Business Process Management
  • Transform your Solution with SAP Cloud ALM

Plus added insights on our Enterprise Architect SAP Community Activities. 

Starting in March key learnings from the 3 1/2 Days of this forum will be shared with the SAP Enterprise Architect Group. 

Stay tuned for post Forum content and conversation in our SAP Enterprise Architecture Group

Join us live on February 27th at any time from 8:30 am to 5:30 pm via this broadcast link:
https://broadcast.sap.com/go/nextgensapea

entry.title: Build Your SAP BTP Tech Radar with LeanIX
entry.content:
Content of an SAP BTP Tech Radar 

In many implementations of a Tech Radar, programming languages, technologies, tools, infrastructure services and other topics relating to the development and operations of IT solutions are discussed. Techniques, frameworks and approaches to modern software development are also important.

There is an outstanding catalog of services, missions and reference architectures for SAP BTP in the SAP SAP Discovery Center. However, this is not aligned and evaluated to the specific requirements of your company. Important criteria for the selection of services can be, for example, the company's own knowledge, security requirements or other solutions that are already in use. It is therefore important to evaluate these and use them to create, communicate and implement your own SAP BTP Tech Radar.

How to build Your SAP BTP Tech Radar 

A single person or a central team should not be the only responsible one for setting up your own tech radar. Rather, it is a collaborative task that experts from different teams and areas of a company should perform together. This includes not only developers from different teams, but also IT administrators, security-, business experts and architects.

This group should meet regularly to review evaluations and, if necessary, add new entries and delete those that are no longer required. The assessment made in this way should not be seen as an evaluation of individual SAP BTP solutions, but rather as a classification for your own company.

Before setting up a radar, you should first consider which areas you want to record. The following categories could be an idea for the BTP:

  • BTP Development & Tools with all services and tools required for development
  • BTP Environments & Techniques to evaluate the various runtime environments, cloud providers and techniques for development and operation
  • BTP PaaS Services for classification of the current 92 BTP platform services
  • BTP SaaS Services as a category for all SaaS solutions relating to the SAP Intelligent Enterprise, such as SAP Order Management foundation  or SAP Sales Cloud 

The rings for the evaluation - these ones could be the following:

  • Hold for things that should no longer be used in new projects
  • Incubating for solutions that may be interesting for innovative solutions or special requirements, but with which the teams have not yet gained much experience
  • Emerging for services that have already been successfully used in development and operation, but where some elements may still be missing to make them completely enterprise ready
  • Mature for things that have been tried and tested several times and are stable in operations, so that they can be used as to-go solutions for new projects

Reasons to build Your own Tech Radar

There are many reasons to carry out your own evaluation and classification of services for your company. These can be economic, legal reasons or based on the knowledge of your teams. Instead of a "centrally prescribed" list, it is a good idea to create and elaborate it together through a collaborative process. On the one hand, this usually improves the content, increases acceptance and on the other hand can be used by agile and independent teams as a starting and reference point for the realization of new projects. By feeding back the experiences from the teams, the list an be further developed by a team of architects

Benefits of LeanIX for your SAP BTP Tech Radar

A number of services used are typically already recorded in LeanIX or can be quickly added via the SaaS catalog, what can save time. The implementation in LeanIX is very simple thanks to a report available in the store. A single point of truth makes it very easy to implement and discoverable for your team members. The most important thing, however, is that this can greatly promote usage, acceptance and transparency by linking the entries to your application, processes and capabilities as well as the SAP BTP Sub Accounts and spaces where the services are used. Manual maintenance in Office solutions or a wiki is often quite time-consuming and quickly generates redundant and outdated information.

Realization in LeanIX

 

After adding the Tech Radar report and creating the tag group in the admin area, you can directly start evaluating the SAP BTP service. Simply select the values in the two tag groups for the category and the ring.

Assessment of a service

You can then check the radar directly under the reports and as well check further details on the entry and also see when the evaluation was made.

SAP BTP Tech Radar Example

Update History

Other use cases

Once you have stored and evaluated the services in LeanIX, you can easily assign them to SAP BTP Sub Accounts (Platform Fact Sheet in LeanIX) and applications and thus easily receive further informations in reports (will be described in a further blog). By maintaining your own or SAP based lifecycle information, you can also identify areas of action for you IT landscape.

In order to get the full power out of your SAP BTP instance, it is important to follow a certain level of governance and guidelines. There is a very starting point with the SAP BTP Guidance Framework.

Your ideas

What are your thoughts on this?

How do you see possible uses?

Does having your own SAP BTP Tech Radar make sense? What are the correct categories?

Who has already implemented their own Tech Radar in the SAP area and what are your experiences with it?

entry.title: 2 Controlling Areas in 1 client
Re: Profit.Segment on account assignment is not available
entry.content:
Hi raymond

  • Activate CO-PA cross-client and client-specific? yes, I have activate CO-PA both cross-client and client-specific
  • Did you set the remated master data (characteristics) -> yes the related characteristics and values fields are build within data structure
  • DId you set the derivation rules -> this I haven't set. is that mandatory for using CO-PA

 

 

 

 

@raymond_giuseppi

entry.title: When there are 2 Controlling Areas in 1 client, should I use 2 Operating concerns?
entry.content:
When there are 2 Controlling Areas in 1 client, should I use 2 Operating concerns?

There are 2 Controlling Areas in 1 client.
However, the currency is different for each.
It should be set to the same currency as the operation concern.....
Should I create and use two Operation Concerns?

<Current settings>
1000 Controlling Area: USD <--> assign 8000 Operation concern
2000 Controlling Area: EUR <--> assign 8000 Operation concern
8000 Operation concern: USD

In this case, should I create an additional Operation Concern (e.g. 8001)?
Or can I use it as is?

entry.title: Re: Join the Livestream of Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum
entry.content: Are you aware that the link to the SharePoint site requires an SAP login?  Is that intended or is the site supposed to be public?

entry.title: Re: A new common place to find SAP Reference architecture diagrams from the community
entry.content:
Hi @UxKjaer,

Rather than providing multiple places to search for reference architectures, I would suggest that if possible, it would make more sense to use this new GitHub site to complement the Discovery Centre by enabling the contribution pipelines to also update the Discovery Centre Reference Architectures Catalogue.

Regards,

Mustafa.

entry.content:
Hello everybody,
I have the following error when trying to create a package in Eclipe connected to SAP BTP: System setting does not allow changes to be made to object DEVC.

Can someone help me please?

Thanks

entry.title: Exceedance error in manifest.yml file config - Cloud Foundry BTP
entry.content:
I'm trying to deploy a Python Application using the Cloud Foundry CLI in a trial BTP account. When I try to push the project, the terminal shows me the following message as output:

memory quota_exceeded, memory instance_memory_limit_exceeded, app_instance_limit app_instance_limit_exceeded

I have tried every way to change the "manifest.yml" file, but in every attempt I'm warned that a certain service has exceeded the trial account limit.

How should I proceed?

entry.title: Re: Join the Livestream of Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum

entry.title: Re: How to call a search help by ABAP code in dynpro module?
entry.content: Thanks for your comment.

entry.title: Re: Profit.Segment on account assignment is not available
entry.content:
updated comment

I have positioned the direct reason for the PA segment editing issue.  when the WBS element is with values, the PA segment is not availble, while I cleard the value on the WBS element, the PA segment CAN be edited with a pop-up window. 

it seemed the WBS element was conflicted/not compatible with the PA segment. so please give an further guidance how to solve this?

thanks in advance.

@raymond_giuseppi

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2402
entry.content:
As promised the most recent Sydney Stammtisch was at a new venue, The Orient Hotel in The Rocks

I thought it worked well. It was pleasant to be on the terrace, easy to get served at the adjacent bar and it was easy to chat because the music wasn't too loud

But what do you think?  If you came along let us know in the comments if we should pick this venue again in future

I found the evening quite relaxing because we had a good turnout almost right from the start.  We had quite a few regulars (where would we be without them?) but also some new faces, which is terrific. I didn't really get to chat to everyone as much as I would have liked, but then there is always next time...

..likely that will be in May, after the next school hols.  Add a comment below if you have venue suggestion for the next event

#SAPStammtischSYD

entry.title: Re: how to implement multiple gst rates for HSN based GST...
entry.content: Hi, I was mentioning about material group - if you can create multiple material groups and distinguish with description, the user shall select it while creating PO with non-codified items and tax can be triggered based on material group added as part of condition table.

entry.title: Re: How to download Material Pallet information for a shipping account or for all the materials?
entry.content: Hi, kindly let us know which field in material master you are referring to.

entry.title: Re: How to look up plants in SAP S4
entry.content:
Hi,

You may check EC03 - structure - Navigation, which will get you the org structure. If you need list of plants, you may check T001W table.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Condition records picking twice in Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi,

It looks like the conditions are copied from last purchase order - you may check the following:

1. In the PO line item level, if info-record is present, please remove it manually and check whether the duplicate JIIG is appearing.

2. Check the OMFI settings and ensure that the copy from last PO is not maintained in the default values key. You need to assign the key in the user profile - parameter ID with parameter ID EVO and then test again. Note: you may also create a default value with blank key, so that the parameter assignment is not mandatory.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2402
entry.content: It was a great event, thanks Mike for organizing.   The venue was very good, it was not noisy which is good for conversation.

entry.title: Re: Building a Custom SAP Gateway Service with SEGW
entry.content:
Yeah, this approach was all the rage back around 2016. It is still valid in ECC but there are dozens of blog posts already on this subject and it is absolutely not the best way to develop "Gateway services" in later S/4HANA systems. Yet this post somehow doesn't mention this fact...

Here is just one example from 2016 where this concept is explained in great detail: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/odata-service-development-with-sap-gateway-code-based-service-development/ba-p/13232029

Dear readers, please don't use this in S/4HANA unless you've exhausted other options.

Dear blog writers, please consider not covering this subject anymore in 2024. Let's move on.

Thank you.

entry.title: Attach excel sheet on Selection screen
entry.content:
Hi,

I have an ABAP prog(SE38) that take excel file as input. In order to let the user know the format of the input excel sheet to the program, I need to attach an excel sheet as template on selection screen.

How do i create & attach this excel sheet on selection-screen.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK bind data to the List picker control from the...
entry.content: How and where are you populating the first list picker that is bound to a string collection?

entry.title: Duplicate Batch No Notification
entry.content:
Hi

  I have below Notification for Duplicate Batch No but i want that it should not be check if User Cancels the Document.

  IF @object_type ='20' and @transaction_type in ('A')
BEGIN
if exists(Select distinct 'Error' from IBT1 B1 , IBT1 B2
where b1.BatchNum in (isnull(b2.BatchNum,'')) and B1.BaseEntry = @list_of_cols_val_tab_del
and b1.ItemCode = B2.ItemCode and b2.BaseEntry <> @list_of_cols_val_tab_del and b2.BaseType = 20 and b1.BaseType = 20)
begin
SET @error = 2000
SET @error_message = 'Duplicate Batch No. not allowed!'
End
End

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAC story design exam(C_SACS_2321) expiring soon, wha...
entry.content:
@Debar_23 

SAC is an evolving product with features being released every Quarter. To keep pace with the new features the SAC exam keeps getting updated and the previous one is expired at regular intervals. When you do a certification its valid for certain years even if that exam is retired. You could take the new exams if you are interested to upskill yourself but your previous exam remains valid till its validity date. If you are planning to take the SAC exam first time its better to wait for the new exam release as you can certify yourself on the latest version of the exam.

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: Last Purchase Price
entry.content:
Hi
      I have purchased item but in Last Purchase Price it is showing 0. In Item Master Last Purchase Price column it is showing 0 . I created new Invoice also then it is not getting updated.
 
Thanks

entry.title: Details of Customers' Certificates is made mandatory in customer master template migration cockpit.
entry.content:
Dear All,

While uploading the customer master data in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud I saw the sheet with name "Certificate Details", and it's been marked as mandatory. 

The screenshot of the definition of certificate I have attached for your ref.

I have also gone through the SAP Help portal; there it mentioned that for India it's been marked as mandatory. The address linked I have added here for reference. - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/b251af240d9c41e9b9baf94603b9491a.html?locale=en-US&q=business%20partner%20certificate%20india

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/38118c6119ec4bfcb43ff5bbec1fd818.html?locale=en-US&q=business%20partner%20ceryificate%20indis

So, how to upload of customer master data successfully using migration cockpit If I don't have any "Certificate Details" from the client end for customer master?

Any help is appreciated!  

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit SAP S/4HANA business partner

entry.title: Re: Percentage and absolute value in same measure column on bar chart
entry.content:
@MananKotak 

SAC format is either inherited from Account dimension or Measures. If priority is set to Accounts and Dimension with A , B and C is an account dimension you could set different formatting for Account members A, B and C in Account dimension. But if this dimension is any other type then its not possible. Measure can have only single format either % or decimal. Also values whether % or decimal are stored in decimal format only, its just the display format which gets adjusted according to the formatting set for the account or measure. Another thing is if you show both % and integer on a bar chart the scale will get distorted as 45 and 0.08 are completely different scale.

 

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Date Dimension in SAC
entry.content:
@Vasu_Sac 

You need to provide all months as selection by selecting them in the filter for date and set it to single selection.

Date Filter

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference

SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference is back! Interact with SAP and partners at Seville, Spain for an immersive experience where industry leaders, experts, and innovators will converge to explore the latest trends, challenges, and opportunities in transitioning to smart and sustainable solutions within the Energy and Utilities sector.

Link to event: Official TAC Website

entry.title: Re: Demystifying Joule - SAP´s New Generative AI Assistant
entry.content: Thanks for the valuable insights!

entry.title: Enabling SSO for SAC with Corporate Idp (AZURE AD) via Cloud Identity Service
entry.content:
Let's begin by listing all applications involved in this setup:

  1. Access to SAP Analytics Cloud as System Owner.
  2. Access to Cloud Identity Service as an Administrator.
  3. Access to AZURE AD or contact person who have access to Azure AD.

Now let’s understand how the connection will be established among three applications.

  1. Need to establish trust between Cloud Identity Service and Azure AD (Corporate Identity Provider) by exchanging metadata among themselves and registering one another at their platform.
  2. Then, we will establish trust between Cloud Identity Service and SAP Analytics Cloud by exchanging metadata among themselves and registering one another at their platform.

From above 2 lines its clear that Cloud Identity Service acts as a proxy between SAP Analytics Cloud and Azure AD.

PART 1

Let’s begin by configuring trust between SAP Cloud Identity Service and AZURE AD

 

1. Login to your Cloud Identity Service Portal.

 

2. Go to “Application and Resources” and from drop down select “Tenant Settings.”

 

3. Click on “Single Sign-On” tab.

 

4. Click on “SAML 2.0 Configuration”.

 

5. Click on “Download Metadata File".

 

6. Once you have the metadata file downloaded as below. Ask your AZURE AD team to upload metadata and setup SSO for your Cloud identity service. Once the setup is done, they will share metadata which they will have post configuration.

 

7. Once you receive metadata file from AZURE AD team, go again to your Cloud Identity Service Portal and under “Identity Providers” drop down select “Corporate Identity Providers”.

 

8. Click on "Create" .

 

9. Provide the Display name as you want to and select “Microsoft ADFS/ AZURE AD (SAML 2.0)“.Then check on "Create".

 

10. Now you can see an entry under Corporate Identity Providers. Select “AZURE AD” which you have created in the last step.

 

11. Click on “SAML 2.0 Configuration” and click on “Browse” and upload the metadata file which you have received from Azure AD team post configuration of Cloud Identity service at their end.

 

12. After uploading metadata all fields will get populated automatically. Now click on "Save".

 

😎TRUST BETWEEN CLOUD IDENTITY SERVICE AND AZURE AD is Done 😎

PART 2

Now we will configure trust between SAP Cloud Identity Service and SAP Analytics Cloud

 

1. Login into you SAP Analytics Cloud Portal as SYSTEM_OWNER role and then click on “System” and Click on "Administration”.

 

2. Click on tab “Security".

 

3. Click on pencil icon to enable edit mode.

 

4. Switch the Authentication Method from "SAP Cloud Identity (default)" to "SAML Single Sign-On (SSO)".

 

5. Click on "Download". You will now have SAP Analytics Cloud metadata downloaded.

 

6. Click on the "Cancel" icon to discard the current changes and revert to "SAP Cloud Identity (default)" authentication for now.

7. Login into SAP Cloud Identity Service Portal, under “Applications and Resources” drop down click on “Applications”.

 

8. Click on Create.

 

9. Provide a Display name as per your convenience, select "SAML 2.0" radio button and click on Create.

 

10. Click on Application which we have added in last step.

 

11. Click on “SAML 2.0 Configuration” then click on Browse and upload the metadata file which you have downloaded from SAP Analytics Cloud. All details will get auto populated and then click on Save.

 

12. Click on “ Subject Name Identifier” and select basic attribute and fallback attribute , in our case we have selected email and User Id respectively , because we use email id as Name Identifier to authenticate from AZURE AD. Now click on Save.

 

13. Click on “Default Name Id Format” select “Email” radio button and click on Save.

 

14. Click on “Apply Function to Subject Name Identifier” in our case we have selected Lowercase because our user’s which are created in SAP Analytics Cloud have their email Id maintained ,all in lowercase. Click on Save.

Reference Snote : 2824009

15. Click on “Conditional Authentication” and under “Default Identity provider” drop down select the Idp which you have configured earlier under “Corporate Identity Provider” (Display name which you have provided will be visible). Click on Save.

 

16. Now Login into SAP Analytics Cloud portal as SYSTEM OWNER authorization.

17. Click on “System” and then Click on "Administration”.

 

18. Click on tab “Security”.

 

19. Click on pencil icon to enable edit mode.

 

20. Switch the Authentication Method from "SAP Cloud Identity (default)" to "SAML Single Sign-On (SSO)".

 

21. Click on ‘Upload’ to upload the metadata.xml file (which you have downloaded in Part 1 Step 5 of this document) of our Cloud Identity Service and click ‘OK’.

 

22. Under Step 3 select the user attribute to map to our IdP. Select ‘Email’ .

 

23. Under Step 4 provide an email id which exists in AZURE AD and click on verify account.

 

24. "Login URL" is displayed. Use the ‘Copy’ icon to select and copy the URL and paste it into a private browser session (incognito mode).

 

25. Once the private browser session displays the message below, the "SAML account verification was successfully".

 

26. Go to SAP Analytics Cloud Screen and click on “Check Verification”.

 

27. Click "Save" to save the configuration changes.

 

28. Click "Convert" to change the authentication method to SAML single sign-on.

 

Finally we have successfully configured SSO for SAP Analytics Cloud with AZURE AD via Cloud Identity Service.

You can now login into SAP Analytics Cloud using SSO with your corporate credentials.

entry.title: Reg : S4 Internal Price calculation CPQ
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Mapped the VPRS condition type under the pricing condition mapping with step counter 940 to the quote custom field within CPS. While the value for the VPRS condition type was successfully copied from the valuation segment of the material master in S4, it's not being determined correctly in CPS.

I assume, correct me if I am wrong, the reason could be that the functionality doesn't work due to the runtime calculation and determination not being based on the condition stored record value in CPS.

Does this need to be addressed through a pricing extension?

I have found the required attribute, but I am not sure how to use it in "General Attribute mapping"."

entry.title: Groovy script udf to handling script length
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have mapping requirement to check for the length of the  string of a array field. If it is a greater than 13, trim it to 13 and if it is empty or less than 13, send as it is.

I tried to write a code as below , it is not working. I'm getting the error.

 

The root message is: Exception:[com.sap.aii.mappingtool.tf7.rt.BehaviorInvocationException: java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: while trying to load from index 1 of an object array with length 1, loaded from local variable 'a'] in class com.sap.aii.mappingtool.tf7.ScriptHandler method StringLen[[Ljava.lang.String;@6a975eaf, com.sap.aii.mappingtool.tf7.rt.ResultListImpl@27bbd186, com.sap.xi.mapping.camel.impl.MappingContextImpl@68693cd6, com.sap.aii.mappingtool.tf7.rt.Context@79fb94ab] on the exchange: Exchange[D10647C5A5BE62C-0000000000000003]

 

 

import com.sap.it.api.mapping.*;
def void StringLen(String[] arg1, Output output, MappingContext context)
{
def str = arg1[0]
for (int i = 0; i < str.length(); i++)
if(str[i].length() >= 13)
{
output.addValue(arg1[i].substring(0,12))
}
else
{
output.addValue(arg1[i])

}
}

entry.title: Re: Shipping to Vendor for repair and return
entry.content: Thanks JagdeepSingh. Yes my equipment is serialized component. Can you please eleborate the steps required as i am bit confused. Regular Purchase Requisition created against the local job order(work order) but without material number--> Regular PO using the same created PR --> 541 movement in MIGO to ship to vendor --> Shipping the physical component to vendor through ZLO-DT/Shipment --> Receiving the physical unit back from the vendor --> Perform GR(using MB1B-101) for the PO(initially created without material number). Then the serial number will reflect in my stock location (OR) do i need to perform separate movement to bring the unit in to my stock? Please correct me if i am wrong. Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: BATCH LOCKED Issue (VL02N OR MIGO)
entry.content: Opting for Batch Splitting is the optimal solution for your inquiry. This involves dividing a batch into smaller sub-batches, allowing different users to work on different portions simultaneously.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Batch No Notification
entry.content:
Hi ramco1917,

For Cancel, include @transaction_type = 'C' accordingly in the notification script.

Hope it helps!

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: My journey about SAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I have been studying SAP-related content recently.

  I want to join the community to document my learning experiences and thoughts. I have been studying the MM module and relevant operations of ABAP.

  Through learning SAP, I currently feel the rigor of SAP, requiring patience to gradually log in and confirm various settings. However, due to a lack of practical project experience, it is still difficult for me to understand what kind of actual business problems SAP is solving.

entry.title: Re: How to look up plants in SAP S4
entry.content: If you wish to access information about plants and their corresponding storage locations at the configuration level, please refer to OX09 tcode. For table-level, SE16N > T001L table.

entry.title: Re: My journey about SAP
entry.content: If you can tell me how to quickly get started with SAP, I would be very happy. It would be best if there is Chinese or Japanese. because my English is not very good, and I rely on translation software.

entry.title: Subcontracting process process Accounting entry not coming correctly
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I got a query from the users in subcontracting process accounting entry;

Raw material worth 50 issued for sub contracting, sub contracting charges = 500, the goods receipt of SFG product should come 500+50=550 but its coming 500 for the semi finished goods (SFG) which is receiving. The SFG material price control maintained as Standard (S). 

This is the requirement of the user, anyone please help us with the solution for this.

Thanks in advance,

Vignesh

SAP S/4HANA for financial products subledger EWM - Goods Movement SAP Invoice and Goods Receipt Reconciliation SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Finance SAP Pricing and Costing for Utilities FIN Material Ledger MM (Materials Management) FIN General Ledger

entry.title: Re: How to call a search help by ABAP code in dynpro module?
entry.content: Please do not post a solution for commenting the question. Use the link "Show replies" below the question and then "Comment".

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - Permission Error
entry.content: A silly request, but can check that the roles are inside the role collection?

entry.title: Re: Attach excel sheet on Selection screen
entry.content: Store the Excel template anywhere (many ways) + one button on selection screen "Download Excel template"? Many questions and answers already posted in the forum.

entry.title: Re: Functions Issue (By Me) Tab
entry.content:
I created a flow function with just JavaScript node inside and put this flow function in 2 different places, and it worked as expected. If I update one of them only that is updated. If I then click overwrite local template all instances are updated.

Maybe give some more details of what's happening in the custom flow function.

entry.title: Re: How do I get and set x-csrf-token and set-cookie in S...
entry.content:
Hi Naoto,

Did you try using the CSRF feature in Action Project settings(on top right of Actions Editor). Once this is switched on then for all modifying calls ( POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE) an automatic GET call is made behind the scenes to fetch the CSRF token and the cookie.

With this feature you need not make a separate GET call to fetch the CSRF token and cookie.

If this does not solve the issue, please raise an incident in component CA-AP-ACT, it requires a closer look from SAP Development team.

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: PAGE/APP Variable issue
entry.content: That's a bug, definitely ... not only can't you select the variable but the whole page stops working. Refreshing the page brings it back. It breaks no matter the name of the object property.

entry.title: Re: Create a oData server and add multiple entity
entry.content: Is this related to SAP Build?

entry.title: Comments in SAC excel addin
entry.content:
Hi

I want to use the comment functionality to create a collaboration between users, on SAC Excel Add-in, however when I close the excel - the comments are lost  - id there a way to keep them?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to change 'Started By' field value in SAP BPA process Instance
entry.content:
Are you starting is from Build Apps or using a destination?

Instead of setting the destination with OAuth2ClientCredentials authentication you use OAuth2UserTokenExchange authentication. No other changes are required.

What can I do?

entry.title: Re: Comments in SAC excel addin
entry.content:
@dd5 

Currently the SAC Add-in has no comment support. You can follow the below influence request and as per the latest comment there 2024 Q2 might have some comment related formula to get and set comments.

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

 

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Builds Apps using BTP Destination - 500 Internal...
entry.content:
This would probably work well if it were registered and called in the SAP Work zone (standard or advance)
like this https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/workzone-build-6-sales-app.html

entry.title: Re: Advance Script Slow Performance in SAC for Planning D...
entry.content:
@edwardcs 

If you add an attribute to your account dimension say SRC_ACC and maintain FI_CALCOPS_0013 as value against FI_CALCOPS_0016 and so on for other similar account reads, you could replace the first 4 DATA() and RESULTLOOKUP pairs to just 1.

DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/MID_ACCOUNT_LT_PT] = [d/MID_ACCOUNT_LT_PT].[p/SRC_ACC])

 

 

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: ACDOCA-MATKL_MM is always empty & i want to fill it.
entry.content:
I am using S/4 HANA, I found that ACDOCA-MATKL_MM is always empty.

How to fill it with any FI document is created on the system.

also I have tried GGB1 - Substitution Maintenance & I wasn't able to find MATKL_MM.

if you have any other options/solutions this would be great.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Regarding SAP Build Apps Authentication issue
entry.content:
If you have created destination as described in Create Destination to Trigger Process From any Service / Step 3 , the url of the destination may be wrong. Use the url from SAP Build Lobby / Control Tower / Unattended Triggers/ [Your Process Trigger] / View (refer to: Run the Sales Order Business Process / Step 3 )

Also: It is better to create the destination with Authentication as OAuth2JWTBearer. Then "Started By" for tasks will get correct value.

entry.title: Re: Talent intelligence hub - 2024 updates and behind the scenes!
entry.content: Thanks a lot for sharing the detailed info. It would amazing to know How integration with OCN's and their role and skills libraries would be used in SF learning ? Will skills and other attributes used by OCN be mapped to a course/item in SF learning OR will it be required to first import the attribute library(skills or any such attributes) used by OCN in TIH-attribute library and then when it becomes available in learning through sync be allowed to be mapped with item

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content: For the moment, you can get the slides here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-codejam/blob/working-2.0/2024-Slides.pdf

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps composer pro feature requests
entry.content: Do you mean when you deploy an app natively to the app store / Google Play -- you get a request for camera permission? Why shouldn't someone have to give permission to use the camera?

entry.title: Re: Java Script in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: I'm not familiar with the Firebase use case (not the primary use case for SAP Build Apps anymore, it seems). I don't know if it will be related, but there seems to be a new version mostly surrounding BTP authentication available on mobile -- already available on web -- and this may help you. I would imagine another month. Of course, others using Firebase may be able to help sooner.

entry.title: Re: How to Call OData Functions from SAP Build Apps
entry.content: @Akaay Can you explain exactly what you are looking for?
